task_yaml.task:
I am organizing paper-reading notes for the RAG evaluation benchmark area, and
I want a desktop GUI workflow with reviewable evidence.

Use real desktop applications to complete the task: Zotero, Obsidian, and
LibreOffice Calc. Do not use local replacement web tools, and do not only use
scripts to generate the final files directly. The task environment runs an
install/verification service first; if the applications are still installing,
check `/tmp_workspace/clawbench/service_state/task_105_04_real_apps_state.json`
and wait until the status is `ready` before launching the applications.

1. Open and read `/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/papers/ares_rag_evaluation.pdf`
   in a real PDF reader or browser PDF viewer. Confirm the paper title,
   authors, year, main task, main metric or metric dimensions, and one
   limitation. Save a screenshot showing that the PDF is open:
   `/tmp_workspace/results/pdf_reader_ares.png`.
2. In real Zotero, create one literature record. Fill in at least title,
   authors, year, and publication, and attach that PDF path to the record. Save
   a Zotero record or item-details screenshot to
   `/tmp_workspace/results/zotero_ares_record.png`. The screenshot must make
   the attachment auditable as belonging to the same ARES parent item, for
   example by expanding the parent item to show the child attachment, or by
   showing `ares_rag_evaluation.pdf`, a PDF icon, a paperclip/attachment count,
   or equivalent nonzero attachment evidence in the same item details area. A
   bibliographic record with no attachment is not enough; proving only that the
   PDF exists in the filesystem is not enough. Export that Zotero record to
   `/tmp_workspace/results/zotero_ares_record.bib` or
   `/tmp_workspace/results/zotero_ares_record.ris`; the export should include
   the attachment path or local PDF filename when possible.
3. In a real Obsidian vault, create a note named `RAG Benchmark Note.md`. The
   content must include these three subsections:
   - What task does it benchmark?
   - What metric matters most?
   - One limitation
   Save or export the note to
   `/tmp_workspace/results/rag_benchmark_note_export.md`, and save an Obsidian
   note screenshot to `/tmp_workspace/results/obsidian_rag_benchmark_note.png`.
   The screenshot should show the opened note title and at least two required
   subsections. Do not save only the Obsidian startup page, folder selector, or
   an empty vault.
4. In real LibreOffice Calc, save `/tmp_workspace/results/rag_reading_tracker.ods`
   with these columns:
   Title | Year | Main Task | Main Metric or Metric Dimensions | Worth reading further (yes/no)
   Save a Calc screenshot to `/tmp_workspace/results/calc_rag_reading_tracker.png`.
   The screenshot should show real LibreOffice Calc with the tracker open, and
   should make columns A through E and row 2 readable in the same image,
   especially `Worth reading further (yes/no)` and its `yes` or `no` value.

All screenshots, Zotero exports, ODS, and Markdown files must be under
`/tmp_workspace/results/`. Do not only copy the abstract; the note and table
should reflect your judgment from reading the PDF. Do not use scripts to
directly generate or prefill the final Markdown, citation export, ODS, or
screenshots as a substitute for the final state in real Zotero, Obsidian, and
LibreOffice GUI applications. Final screenshots must separately prove the
final state in those real applications.
